desc:
The road twists through the scenic landscape, following a rough north to east
direction. To the south and west, a muddy embankment leads down to the leisurely
flowing river, winding its way through the countryside.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Standing in the middle of the road you look around, surveying the surroundings.
To the north, vast fields and plains spread out across the horizon, as far as
the eye can see. South and west, the road continues its path through the
countryside, while to the east, a gravel path leads up to a nearby tavern.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
A scattering of trees marks the edge of the road as it turns north and east,
mirroring the path of the Nen Kalthar. Splashes of colour from the surrounding
countryside, set against the vibrant river to the west, create a picturesque
landscape.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here is cracked and broken in places, partly due to the expansive roots
from the surrounding trees, but also to the erosion caused by a small trickle of
water that flows across the road from a nearby spring. The trees looming
overhead cast long shadows across the road and the only sound to be heard is the
constant burble of water into the spring.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Partially sheltered by the bordering trees, the road bends around to the south
and west. The road itself is very worn here, and rutted tracks filled with
rainwater run along the roadside. Several large rocks have been recently placed
in the middle of the road making an effective roadblock for any passing coaches.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here has fallen into disrepair, despite the fact that it is obviously
used quite frequently. Perhaps as people grow closer to the city of thieves,
they also grow less willing to tarry on the open road, where unseen eyes could
easily spy upon their movements. The thoroughfare continues north and south from
here.
name: A Long Straight Stretch of the Road

desc:
The hard-packed dirt road is rutted and a few rocks and stones litter the way,
making it rather a perilous track to follow in any wheeled vehicle. Where
further north, and closer to Bree, the road had been well-maintained, here it
has been neglected for quite some time, judging by its appearance.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road turns here, from west to south, as if trying to draw closer to the
river. To the east it is bordered by a vast expanse of flatlands, but to the
north a rough hedgerow of wild-looking bushes blocks the way, and the view,
hiding whatever lies beyond from your sight.
name: On a Winding Roadway

desc:
Just south of here a small stony path diverges from the road, then itself splits
to lead both south and west. The main thoroughfare continues north and east,
following its seemingly endless route between Tharbad, southeast of here, and
the distant village of Bree, far to the north.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Deep holes and ruts scar the road, as it scars the countryside in turn, cutting
a path between Bree and Tharbad. Northward and eastward, the road is bordered by
an open plain, affording no relief from the wind and weather.
name: A Deeply Rutted Road

desc:
The road journeys north and east from here, and is bordered to the west by a
wild and unkempt hedgerow of thick and thorny shrubs. The surface of the roadway
is in poor condition, ill-suited to travel in any wheeled vehicle, due to
innumerable potholes and deep ruts, which now criss-cross the hard- packed dirt.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
The road here moves east-west, bordered on the north by grassy plains and by a
stony bank in the south. Neither provide shelter or any form of relief from the
summer sun, or from winter storms. The road itself is made of hard-packed dirt,
and has fallen into some disrepair in this part of the country.
name: Road to Tharbad

desc:
Not far to the south, the forbidding gates of Tharbad can be seen, tall and
imposing. The road here is made of interlocking granite slabs mortared with
hard-packed dirt, and to either side of the road tall smooth walls climb high,
as if funneling travellers toward the city gates. A heavy door of stout oak
planks is set into the eastern wall, presumably leading to some sort of guard
hut. Any traveller trapped here would be at the mercy of the denizens of
Tharbad.
name: Approaching the North Gate of Tharbad

desc:
You are in front of a huge wooden gate. On the city wall, beside the gate, you
notice a big sign. To the north, the road leads to the Shire, far away.
name: Outside the North Gate of Tharbad

desc:
You are walking along a narrow, flower-lined path. To the north you can enter a
peaceful glade, full of sweet-scented flowers and plants. To the east you can
see a large green hedge enclosing a fairly large area, but you can't see what it
is protecting from here. To the south-east you can see a wooden hut outlined
against some green hills in the background.
name: A Small Path

desc:
The path here is narrow and lined with flowers. It runs from the north to the
south, where it then bends to the east. To the east of here you can see some
wisps of smoke coming from the chimney of a small wooden hut, and beyond that
you can see some small hills. Far to the north you can see a sparkle of water,
and right at the range of vision there seems to be a green smudge which could be
a forest of some description.
name: A Small Path

desc:
From here you can clearly see that the path is leading to the wooden hut just
east of here, and then it continues on up the gentle slopes of the hills beyond
it. To the north-east you see a green hedge fencing something in, but you cant
see through it. Small boulders line the path here, interspersed with flowers and
coloured pebbles. To the west a smaller path leads toward a towering, and
seemingly quite ancient, forest.
name: An Intersection of Paths

desc:
The path here leads east and west. The path is lined by borders of dark, rich
soil which has a fresh peaty smell. Far north of here you can see a sparkle of
water, and beyond that the dark green of a forest. Close at hand you can smell
the heady scent of many flowers, and also the faint aroma of cooking.
name: A Small Path

desc:
The path here leads past a small wooden hut. The construction does not seem to
be sophisticated, and yet the building seems quite sound. Flowering ivy climbs
the walls, neatly framing the two windows which look out over this pathway, and
roses border the structure, many different varieties blooming and growing in a
harmony of colours, giving off a sweet perfume. To the right of the stout wooden
door, a small golden plaque has been attached to the wall.
name: A Path in Front of a Hut

desc:
Flowing from north to south, the river spills out into a wide channel as it
emerges from the shadow of the gully. The high rocky banking on the western side
prevents any progress in that direction, but it should be possible to pull
yourself up to the muddy banking on the eastern side.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river bends around a pebbled shoreline, widening out as it flows eastwards.
The ice cold water is quite turbulent here as it flows over the rocky seabed,
accompanied by a low roar. Through the crystal clear water, you can see many
silvery fish darting about.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river is quite shallow here as its path widens out to the soft, muddy banks
that flank it on either side. Meandering slowly south, the undertowing current
is nevertheless quite strong. The low embankment to the north and east provide a
muddy scramble to the dry land.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Coursing from north to east here, the river flows ever onward, its waters
cutting into the low banks to the south, as they meander past. To the west, a
pebbly shoreline reaches down to the water's edge, and gentle waves lap against
the land, tossing some of the tiny stones back and forth. A fat carp swims here.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river takes another twist here on its endless journey south. As the current
swirls around, broken roots and stones are washed onto the northern bank.
Looking downstream, the smashed remains of a small rowing boat lie beached at
the river's edge.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
The river makes a turn from north to east here. Although quite calm, the waters
are deep, and too wide to jump across, so that the only way to get from one side
to the other without a boat would be to swim. Small, silvery fish dart here and
there, but somehow always manage to vanish from sight before they can be seen
closely.
name: At a Bend in the River

desc:
Here the river turns from the west to the south, and the current picks up a
little pace. Small animals scurry along the shores, but the banks are high and
steep, so these creatures quickly vanish from sight. Even the flat moss- covered
rock, which dips its leading edge into the water, further to the north, now
stands at some height above the river, as the channel cuts deeper into the land.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
Turning from west to south, the swift river flows past the steep riverbanks
covered with grass and leafy weeds that hang over the banks into the stream. The
channel is growing wider here, and a number of massive boulders break the
surface of the water.
name: On a Rocky River

desc:
The river flows from the north to the east. The current is stronger here, but it
is still not so strong as to force you to follow the direction of flow, if you
do not choose to do so. The banks to the south and west are steep and slippery -
quite impossible to scale - and the view in those directions is all but obscured
by the river bank. However, a tall and ancient stone tower can just be seen
above the river bank.
name: On a Steep-Banked River

desc:
The river continues its twisting, turning course across the countryside, here
bending from the west to the south. The water seems to be quite deep. Luckily
the eastern bank is low enough to climb. Far in the distance, the top of an old
stone building can be seen above the tree-tops of the forest.
name: At a Bend in the River

desc:
The water is deep here, but so clear that the riverbed can still be seen. As the
river flows onwards through the countryside, it makes another sharp turn, this
time from the north to the east. The low banks of the river south of here allow
the river to flood the low grasslands there.
name: Drifting down a Winding Waterway

desc:
The river travels onward across the countryside, turning here from west to
south. Its banks are low, and the land slopes gently down toward the water,
which has cut a wide channel over the years, so that the current is almost too
slow to be observed.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
As the river flows onward toward Tharbad, it also draws closer to the tall,
stone tower, which looms above the tree-tops to the south-west of here. The
banks of the water are steep, muddy and slippery, and quite impossible to scale,
so the only apparent option is to continue to follow the course of the waterway.
name: The Nen Kalthar

desc:
As the river flows south from here, it enters a wide pool. However, the water
still flows onward, and remains fresh and clear, so the riverbed below is still
quite visible. The banks here are low, and in the distance to the west, an old
stone tower looms into view.
name: On the Widening Waters

desc:
The river widens out into a deep and massive pool here, and looking to the east
it is easy to understand why, for there a sturdy dam has been built across the
path of the waterway. The water spills and splashes across the top of it, to
allow the river to continue its journey eastward, but the pool behind the dam
seems calm and very deep.
name: A Wide Pool

desc:
A surprisingly sturdy dam blocks the course of the river here, forcing the
waters to pool behind it. Lazily the river spills over the top of the many
timbers from which the dam has been constructed, continuing their journey
eastward toward Tharbad.
name: On Top of a Dam

desc:
The path within the woods ends at the base of a large tree, or so it would seem.
The forest is so closely knitted that it is difficult to determine where the
path may actually have gone. The sky is blocked slightly from view by the large
trees and their branches with beautiful green and brown leaves.
name: End of the Trail

desc:
The path has led you to the most exquisite rose garden you have ever seen, where
roses of all kinds grow and flower in abundance, creating an impression both
natural and yet carefully planned. The different scents mingle into a heavy
perfume, which has an almost relaxing effect, and all around, delicate trellises
support climbing flowers, whose heads nod in the gentle breeze.
name: The End of the Garden Path

desc:
The smells of many different flowers and shrubs peacefully assail you. The sharp
tang of sap reminds you of the trees offering shade to both plants and the weary
traveller. The path here leads north east or south. In the south it seems to end
under a wooden trellis archway hung with roses and other climbing plants.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
You are standing in the middle of a cosy and well-proportioned room. The
furnishings are simple, but tasteful, and crafted skillfully, to enhance the
beauty of their surroundings. To your right is a fireplace, with a simple stove,
which must be the source of the smoke that could be seen from afar, and a
half-circle of comfortable chairs have been set in front of this, as if to
welcome visitors at any time.
name: Inside a Cosy Hut

desc:
The flowers here are mostly shades of purple and yellow, and seem to be planted
in shapes and patterns which look vaguely familiar. The path leads in all
directions except south, where a large green hedge signifies the end of the
garden. Beyond the hedge you catch a glimpse of a wooden hut. You can hear the
sounds of someone working nearby.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
The path here leads south and west, winding among many beds of flowers and small
stands of trees. Some of the flower beds have been artfully arranged so as to
make patterns, with one immediately adjacent to the path a perfect resemblance
of a small white cat, lazing in a grassy carpet.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
The tall hedge enclosing this marvellous garden surrounds you on three sides
here. Thick and lush, there is no way through this barrier and so you are
obliged to tread the gravel pathway, which leads north from here. However, to
the west, a curl of smoke can be seen riding from a small building which stands
beyond the hedgerow.
name: A Path in a Well-tended Garden

desc:
The hills undulate gently here, rising to the north and dropping to the east and
south. From the west a faint breeze blows, bringing with it the scent of flowers
and freshly cut grass. In the east you can see the hills dropping down to the
bank of a small creek, and beyond this you can see the sparkle of water. Far in
the north you think you can see a dark smudge of green.
name: The Gentle Hills

desc:
The hills here stretch east and south, a rocky slope to the north and to the
west prevent further travel in those directions. However, beyond this slope, to
the west, a high hedge can be seen, beyond which a well-tended garden has been
laid out. Just south of this, a wisp of smoke can be seen, rising from the
chimney of a small wooden hut.
name: The Gentle Hills

desc:
The hills spread south and west here, a steep rock-strewn slope preventing you
from making a descent to the east or north. To the east, a small rocky creek can
be seen, and further distant still, this meets with the Nen Kalthar, which flows
from west to east, across the countryside. Far to the north, beyond a wide
roadway, a large woodland can be seen.
name: The Gentle Hills

desc:
The hills here dip down to a pebbled bank in the east. To the north and west you
can climb the slight slope up the hills, to see what lies beyond. The grass
underfoot is lush and green, and smells as fresh as morning dew. Pretty flowers
grow all around, peeping from under rocks or standing, proud and alone in the
field of green.
name: Sloping Hills

desc:
Brightly coloured pebbles and small rocks comprise the shoreline of a narrow
inlet, whose calm waters seem fresh and clear. To the west, a small trail leads
into some low, rolling hills, whilst east of here a range of high hills climb
far above you.
name: On the Bank

desc:
To the north, the Nen Kalthar makes its way aross the countryside, flowing ever
eastward. However, the waters of this small, rocky inlet are all but motionless,
and yet still fresh and clear. The footing is made somewhat treacherous by the
large rocks and small pebbles which cover the bed of this creek.
name: Rocky Creek

desc:
The tranquil waters of this small, rocky creek are clear and fresh, despite the
fact that there is almost no current here. To the south, a sandy pebbly shore
gently upward, offering easy access to the rolling hills beyond, whilst to the
northeast, this tiny inlet joins the waters of the Nen Kalthar.
name: Rocky Creek

desc:
You are walking along some softly sloping hills. The grass underfoot is lush and
green, and pretty flowers grow in abundance. The slope goes down to the west,
leading to a small path, which goes past a wooden hut and then splits into two
parts. To the east, the hill gets steeper, affording a good view all around.
name: Sloping Hills

desc:
Low hills rise and fall, to the north and west of here, whilst to the east a
range of higher hills looms above you. The hillside here is carpeted in lush,
green grass, and a few small flowers, scattered here and there, add a splash of
colour to the scene.
name: Sloping Hills

desc:
Far to the north, you can glimpse the sparkling waters of the Nen Kalthar, as it
meanders across the land, on its endless journey toward the ocean, but from
here, no sound of its splashing waters can be heard. The howl of the wind is
dominant on this high hillside, where a faint trail leads onward, east and
south.
name: Walking in the High Hills

desc:
You stand here in the shadow of the highest hill in the vicinity. Rising tall
behind you it looks as though it could afford a good view of your surroundings.
Even from here, you can see the silver ribbon of the Nen Kalthar stretching from
the north-east to several leagues east of here. A faint path goes east and west
here, leading upwards towards the peak of the hill behind you.
name: Walking in the High Hills

desc:
The ground here is grassy and firm, but rocks are strewn about, making it rather
risky to walk here without paying attention to your path. However, a clear trail
can be seen, which hugs the lower slopes of one of the tallest hills, as it
makes its way west and south across the countryside from here.
name: Walking in the High Hills

desc:
The interior of this small building is as dilapidated as it seemed from the
outside. Small chinks in the wooden walls let the cold air stream in, and a few
stained patches on the aging floorboards show where water has leaked in through
the roughly thatched roof. But, despite the slight disrepair, it would seem that
this place still sees frequent use, judging by the lack of dust or cobwebs.
name: Inside a Small Cabin

desc:
Although the ground here is grassy and firm, it slopes rather severely in some
places, and is littered with small rocks and stones. A narrow path can be seen,
winding up into the hills to the north, and descending east toward the banks of
a sparkling river.
name: On a Winding Path to the Hills

desc:
A pebbled shoreline stretches down from the grassy fields, where it meets the
river's edge, lapping gently against the slope. The damp shingle slides about
underfoot, leaving skewed footprints behind. Looking across the river, you can
see the Road to Tharbad in the distance, a narrow ribbon running across the
lowlands.
name: By the River's Edge

desc:
Long lush grasses cover this gentle slope, which streches down towards the
shoreline of a small, but lively river. Further north and west from here, the
land rises more steeply, entering a narrow band of hills, but to the east lie
only lowlands, the sprawling delta where the Swanfleet and the mighty river
Hoarwell meet at the city of Tharbad.
name: On a Soft Grassy Slope

desc:
The low banks of the river are covered in deep, thick grass and many wild
flowers, fed by the ever-present waters, which sweep from north to east. Further
south, the land rises slightly, climbing above the level of the water, but to
the north and east all that can be seen are endless lowlands.
name: A Low Bank on the Nen Kalthar

desc:
The banks here stand at quite some height above the flowing waters of the
shining river, which feeds the rich grasslands of this area. Looking across the
waterway, the road to Tharbad looms in the distance, cutting a dark scar across
the countryside, which stretches low and flat as far as the eye can see. Not far
from the shoreline the thick grass has been trampled down, as it approaches the
entrance to a small shack. It would appear that this humble building still sees
regular use.
name: Outside a Small Shack

desc:
This small path leads toward a small, but dark forest to the south. From a
distance the woods did not look dense, but here the trees loom above you, with
an almost threatening air. As it heads south, the path is soon lost from sight
amongst the undergrowth, and it seems that it would be all too easy to lose ones
way among those ancient trees. However, a fainter trail can be seen travelling
north through a lush green hollow.
name: Walking at the Forest Border

desc:
Travelling from east to west, this small stony path leads from the river towards
the border of a small woodland. Some way to the northwest an old building can be
seen, and to the south, at the edge of the woods, many massive tree stumps
litter the land.
name: On a Small Stony Path

desc:
The gravel path coming from the west meets an incredibly large felled tree that
spans the river to the east of here. You can see the old washed-out stone ford
at the bottom of the steep slope beneath you. To the north, the river bank is
steep and muddy, probably impossible to scale from the waters below.
name: Path by the Tree Bridge

desc:
This trail is pleasant to travel upon, and you can see many small, tiny
footprints on the ground. To the north you can see a well-travelled road beyond
a weedy field. The path continues east, and west you can see a large tree bridge
across the Nen Kalthar. The ground slopes down toward the river.
name: On a Path to the River

desc:
Thick dark green weeds grow in abundance here in a wide field that gently slopes
from the road north and east of here toward the river where the banks abruptly
change to a very steep slope. In the south, a path leads toward an old river
crossing.
name: Weedy Field beside the Road

desc:
At some height above the river, the soil on the banks here is nonetheless quite
moist, and the grass and weeds grow thick and strong. Leafy weeds and small
shrubs hang into the river, their branches trailing in the waters that flow
inexorably by, as the river makes its way southeast.
name: On the Riverbank

desc:
Sitting inside the turn of the road from north to east of here, these fields
bear many a footpath, presumably from travellers preferring to travel through
the fields than the road itself. Indeed, the fields are pleasant. The grass
grows thick and healthy in spite of all the wear and abuse it's taken. The sound
of running water south and west of here indicates you're near the river.
name: Fields beside the Road

desc:
The riverbank here is surprisingly gentle and leads up to grasslands east and
north. Looking down the river south of here, you can tell that the banks are
much steeper. At the river's edge, almost flush with the level of the water,
lies a large, flat stone, which is almost completely covered with soft, thick,
green moss.
name: A Mossy Stone

desc:
The ground here forms a steep slope between the river and road above. Thick
clods of earth covered in damp grass make the ground difficult to negotiate and
indeed, several of the smaller trees here are broken and doubled over. No doubt
having served as someone's climbing aid.
name: Rugged Grassland by the River

desc:
As you near the river's edge, the muddy embankment becomes more precarious.
Eroded overhangs skirt the edge of the riverbank, threatening to give way at any
moment. Gazing down, the river looks cool and refreshing.
name: Muddy Embankment

desc:
A mixture of soft mud and pebbles makes the ground quite slippery by the rivers
edge. Most of the banking has collapsed into the river and a solitary tree
remains rooted to the embankment, leaning over the river at an acute angle.
name: Muddy Embankment

desc:
This small stony path, leading from the roadway to the north, splits in two, the
main trail heading west toward the river. Petering out not far to the south, the
lesser track leads towards a grassy meadow.
name: Small Path

desc:
This gently sloped, grassy beach along the shore of the Nen Kalthar seems a
perfect spot to recover after taking a swim in the river that flows west to
south here. The path to cross the river lies just north, and grass grows along
the bank of the road to Tharbad for a few miles east of here.
name: At a Resting Place by the River

desc:
Slightly higher than the path of the road north of here, this grassy bank gives
a good view of the river a few miles to the west and the castle beyond. The bank
becomes rather rocky to the east, but no stones litter the gentle slope south
toward the meadow.
name: On a Grassy Bank by the Road

desc:
Overlooking a turn in the road from north to east, this stony bank provides a
decent view of the surrounding lands. Off in the distance southwest of here, you
can make out a tall tower that peeks above a line of trees to the north of the
tower. Looking south, you see a trail of rocks leading down to a river dam.
name: A Stony Bank beside the Road

desc:
Overlooking the running water of the river west and south, this cozy little
meadow full of short, thin-bladed grass sits in a slight depression behind a
bank to the north. A few scattered stones litter the steep banks above the Nen
Kalthar, and the ground starts to get a little stoney towards the east.
name: A Small Grassy Meadow

desc:
Though some are small and flat, and others larger and more irregular, the myriad
rocks which can be seen peeking up through the grass here seem to have been
gathered together deliberately to form a crude path leading from north to south.
The land itself lies in a slight depression, formed by a bank to the north, and
the meadows lying south, east and west of here.
name: In a Stony Meadow

desc:
To the south, the ground slopes down as it meets the Nen Kalthar, the pleasant
waterway, which cuts its way across the countryside. At this point, the river
has been dammed, creating a wide pool, and a small, gently splashing waterfall,
which makes a musical sound, to be heard even at some distance.
name: Fields by the River Dam

desc:
Since the water from the river continuously feed its soil, the grass here grows
healthy and has a deep green colour to it. Northwest, you can see a wide pool
that collects behind the dam. As a result of the pool, the fields west of here
begin to get a little muddy in their lower lying areas. South, a steep
escarpment resists your advance. The grass continues east, lightening in hue.
name: Grassy Fields South of the Dam

desc:
Because of their position adjacent to the pool north of here that collects water
by the dam and also their low banks, the fields here have become very wet and
muddy. From what you can see, the fields east of here look to be wet also, but
the grasslands west remain dry. A steep escarpment south prevents travel in that
direction.
name: In the Muddy Fields by the Pool

desc:
The vegetation here is flat, green and very pleasant to walk through. Aside from
the weeds in the north and the fact that the ground begins to get a little soggy
in the east, there is nothing unpleasant about this pasture nestled in the bend
of a steep escarpment west and south of here.
name: Lush Green Grasslands

desc:
Thick green grass and scattered bushes and shrubs cover these gently rolling
plains. The land here is rich and fertile, and must once have been home to many,
but now the only sign of habitation is a massive castle, standing to the
northwest.
name: On the Rolling Plains

desc:
Verdant lowland plains stretch in all directions as far as the eye can see,
carpeted in thick green grass, and occasional patches of shrubs. To the
northwest, standing amid the grasslands, is a mighty castle, its high stone
walls and massive tower dominating the view in that direction.
name: Grassy Lowlands

desc:
The castle walls loom high above you, their crumbling stones and sheer height
forbidding any attempt to scale them. Dense tangles of thorny brambles grow
right up to the walls, and the grass and weeds here seem little disturbed by any
traveller, and indeed this makes sense, for the huge building appears to offer
no entrance to this side.
name: At the Castle Wall

desc:
The grass grows thick and healthy here, right up to the castle walls, and an
occasional gutteral croak can be heard, seemingly answered by a shrill
insect-like chirping from deep in the vegetation. Peering upward, it is possible
to see the castle battlements far above, but the crumbling stone walls appear to
offer only the most tenuous of foot and handholds, so that it would seem that
attempting to scale them may not be a wise plan.
name: At the Castle Wall

desc:
The land dips down to the north, so low that the river has breeched its banks
there, but here the land is comparitively dry, but nonetheless rich due to the
ever-present waters of the Nen Kalthar. Westward, the plains continue until they
meet the walls of a large castle, clearly visible in the distance, and to the
east they fail only at the river edge.
name: Upon a Grassy Plain

desc:
Tromping through these dense weeds, you hear the melodic sounds of thousands of
unseen insects performing beautiful symphony for anyone who will listen. These
mesmerizing sounds, barely audible above the rush of the river north of here,
become more noticable by the wide pool in front of the dam in the east. The
weeds thin out south and west, as does the wonderful music.
name: Overgrown Weeds

desc:
The surrounding lowlands are filled with splashing noises, for the the river has
overrun its banks in the north. The grass south of here becomes less damp and
more healthy, and the knee-high grass west of here looks almost like a body of
water as it ripples in the breeze.
name: Flooded Lowlands

desc:
The grasses here are deeper than they might have appeared at a distance, and
travelling through the thick growth is actually quite an arduous task. The long,
delicate stalks of grass bob and sway gently in the breeze, and look like the
rippling waves of a vast green lake. Further to the north the vegetation is less
luxurient, as the terrain climbs somewhat, rising well above the level of the
river to the east.
name: Wading in a Sea of Tall Grass

desc:
The plains here are carpeted in a thick growth of tall grasses, which thrive in
the deep rich soil. Sheltered from the elements by the castle and woodland to
the west, but afforded plenty of sunlight by the otherwise open location, this
area would make a splendid pasture, or fertile farmland.
name: Amid the Tall Grasses

desc:
Though the embankment may be inaccessable from the river to the north and east,
nothing prevents you from jumping into the water but your own will. The tall
grass south of here looks like a body of water rippling in the wind, and the
vegetation here is nice and green, but the fields to the west are dry because
they lack the constant watering of the river.
name: Atop a Steep Embankment

desc:
The land here is cut off from the river to the east, by an embankment, which
rises gently from here, then falls off sharply at the water's edge. Sloping
downward gently to the south, the land regains the level of the waterway, and
the grass there is more luxurient, but here the ground is quite parched, and the
vegetation weak and more sparse.
name: A Field of Dry Grass

desc:
Though a swift river cuts across the land some way to the east of here, this
part of the countryside lies too far from its nourishing waters, and all that
manages to grow here are a few sparse patches of coarse grass. Further west, a
gloomy woodland rises above the plain, its thorny and tangled undergrowth
choking out even the thin grass, and contributing to an unwholesome air, which
hangs heavily over the entire area.
name: The Parched Plain

desc:
A single massive stump stands almost alone, at a distance from the numerous
smaller stumps which can be seen in the surrounding area. This must once have
been a magnificent tree, dominating the land here, and thick sinuous roots break
the surface of the ground all around you. To the west a small tract of woodland
has apparently been spared from the woodsman's axe, and the thick undergrowth
between the trees impedes progress in that direction.
name: At a Very Large Stump

desc:
The banks of the river stand high here, and the ever-flowing waters seem to
undercut the land somewhat. However, the earth is stabilised by a massive
network of thick tree roots, which break the surface of the ground all about
you, offering to trick and trip you with every step. These roots are all that
remain here of a once mighty woodland, but further west numerous huge stumps
litter the countryside.
name: On a High River Bank

desc:
The old gate rises up in front of you. This was the main entrance to the main
castle of Minhiriath, and here all the soldiers marched to battle to fight off
the evil minions of the Witchking. You notice that the castle seems to be
surrounded by a huge and tall wall, guarding it from wild animals and unwanted
visitors. The ground in front of the entrance is covered with huge tiles, but
due to weather and erosion over hundreds of year, they have been broken and the
ground is uneven. To the south you can see an old watchtower, while west you can
see a small path leading up to a small mound.
name: At the Outer Castlegate

desc:
You find yourself inside the outer gate of the castle, standing upon a broad
path, which leads directly to the castle keep. The entrance to the main part of
the castle is set into a massive stone wall, which stands at the far side of a
large, square yard, which opens up before you.
name: Path to the Castle Keep

desc:
Elegant marble steps lead up to two huge black irondoors with alternating
spirals of silver and gold that mark the entrance of the castle. Each door is
four feet wide and seven feet high. The doors slide sideways into the castlerock
along metal tracks, making the doors impossible to force open. The entrance is
built halfway inside the sturdy castle walls, and you notice several murderholes
in the ceiling above you. To the north stands a small guard house, and to the
south the castle forges and armoury can be seen.
name: Gate to the Castle Keep

desc:
The south and west walls of this large but low-roofed building are divided into
pens and stalls, most of which are piled with filth and refuse. However a few of
those nearest to the doors have been kept reasonably clean and seem to be in
daily use. To the east, an arched entryway leads into another part of the
building.
name: The Castle Stables

desc:
The top of the tower is not enclosed, but a turretted wall stands on all sides,
at once protecting, and yet offering a clear view in all directions. To the
west, a small path leads down into a valley, whilst eastward, the castle stands
in view.
name: Atop the Tower

desc:
This tower once served as an old watchpost and its main job was to stand vigil
over the castle gate, checking anyone who claimed to have business to do inside.
When you step in, you realise that not many guards could stay inside here at the
same time. The room is simple, only a massive table made of stone has survived
all the years of desolation here. The guards who sat here had a good overview
over the gate to the north via a couple of huge windows on the northern wall. On
the southern wall you can see a small staircase made of iron.
name: Old Guardpost

desc:
This chamber once served as the castle forges, ringing with the clash of metal
upon cold metal, in the smithying of new weapons, and stout armours. But now,
all lies cold and still, and no labours continue here today. Indeed, all the
room is coated in a layer of dust, grease and grime, bespeaking of many years of
disuse, and the racks which once held shiny plate and bright weapons have long
ago been ransacked, leaving nothing of any value.
name: The Castle Forges and Armoury

desc:
Entering this room, you become aware of the unstable and rotten foundation upon
which this castle has been built. Small spots stain the walls, where water has
constantly dripped into the room, and the floor beneath your feet is damp and
muddy. The air is heavy and stinks of rotting wood and sackcloth. Looted, and
damaged by both time and water, this chamber is useless now.
name: Storage Room and Wine Cellar

desc:
This commodious room must once have been home to almost frenzied activity, in
the preparation of great feasts for the lords of Minhiriath. Now most of the
fireplaces, ranges, and massive stone tables are thick with the grime of years
of disuse. Racks which once held pots and pans, spoons and knives, now hang
askew, and the windows set high in the north and south walls are covered in so
much filth that no natural light can enter.
name: The Castle Kitchens

desc:
This massive hall serves as the main entrance to the castle of Minhiriath. It is
a long oval chamber, which runs from north to south, and is furnished in a
combination of pastels and paisleys, slightly faded by age, yet still in much
better condition than the exterior of the castle would suggest. The floor is
comprised of white marble octagons circumscribed with black marble. On both
walls are huge windows with partly coloured glass.
name: The Entrance Hall

desc:
This is the shadowy Hall of Heroes. The long walls bear many attachments for
lanterns, which cast multiple shadows, dancing all around you, and the entire
passageway is lined with the slashed portraits of the original inhabitants of
the castle, which at first glance appear pretty grim. High on the eastern wall
are a row of tiny square windows, but these are so dirty that even bright sun-
light no longer reaches this place. Far to the southern end of the hall, a
massive stone stair spirals upward, whilst a smaller iron stair can be seen
leading downward, at the opposite end of the corridor.
name: In the Hall of Heroes

desc:
The chamber in which you find yourself is both grand and tasteful, and must once
have served both as a meeting hall and a place for feasting and great
celebrations. At one end of the hall, upon a great dais, stands a throne, and
behind this, the walls are hung with heavy tapestries, depicting the lords of
Minhiriath. A long table of polished white marble stands at the centre of the
room, hinting at the opulent banquets, which must once have been served here.
name: The Great Hall

desc:
This is the old guard barracks used by the lord to maintain safety around the
castle. The barracks was built here to provide a fast way to aid and protect
their master. The barracks seem somewhat desolate now, however you can see a
narrow staircase leading down. You see many small windows here facing in all
directions, offering a good view of the castle and its surroundings.
name: Old Barracks

desc:
Though not large, this room nevertheless holds five cage-like prison cells. Time
has taken its toll here, as in other parts of the castle, for all of the cell
doors are broken, and hanging askew on their rusty hinges. The floor is littered
with old chains, and manacles, left behind when the castle was long ago
abandoned. No natural light can enter this place, for the two small windows, set
high in the southern wall, are quite obscured by grass and weeds, which grow in
the yard outside.
name: Prison Cells

desc:
The first thing you notice when you enter is the mosaic floor. The rest of the
room is covered with grey granite, softened by grey velvet hangings. The walls
are made smooth and on the western wall you can see a map carved in depicting a
complete overview map of Numenor, the island who is now lost in the Great Sea.
It seems like nothing has been touched here for years. You can see dust swirling
up at your feet when you walk around.
name: The Grey Room

desc:
As all lords in Cardolan, so the lords of Minhiriath relied upon a number of
bodyguards, who would gladly lay down their lives for their masters. This small,
and unadorned, chamber was their place of rest, while they guarded the master
through the night, ready to spring to his aid, at but a moment's notice. To this
end, the simple cots, cut into niches in the southern wall, were built not for
comfort, but for utility.
name: Bodyguard's Chamber

desc:
A massive stone stair spirals downward, and odd sounds echo up the stairs from
below. To the west the passageway leads towards the private chambers of the
castle, and it is clear that this narrow corridor was built more for utility
than to impress the many guests to the castle. Yet the cold stone walls are
quite smooth to the touch, and decorated with elaborate carvings, on which
traces of gilding remain, in silent testimony to the great skill and artistry of
the builders.
name: At the Top of a Spiral Stair

desc:
The ceiling is arched gracefully above you and delicately painted in blue. A
soft and warm light fills the room and dances off the pale indigo and azure
tiles forming a simple yet elegant mosaic on the walls. The floor is covered in
large tiles, of two shades of blue, arranged in a simple checkerboard pattern,
and at the very centre of the room a large, perfectly spherical crystal sits
atop a round oak pedestal. A low light emanates from the crystal, and it seems
to be secured so as to be impossible to move.
name: The Blue Vault

desc:
The room is small, and only one little window to the north, making the room
dimly lit even at daytime, just like a bedroom should be like. It is here the
lord of Minhiriath commonly rested, sleeping on a raised platform bed in the
middle of the room.
name: Royal Bedrooms

desc:
This tiny chamber nestles in the rafters of the castle roof, and has a low
ceiling. The walls on three sides of the room are sloped, but the third stands
tall and straight, and is lined with an old wooden bookshelf. Alas, no books, or
other items of interest remain upon it. The only exits from this attic are to
return down to the corridor below, or to investigate the room, which lies
through an opening in the straight wall, to the west.
name: Secret Chamber

desc:
Thirty feet above the plains by the river, these battlements serve as a lookout
tower for the castle. Scanning north, you can see the Road to Tharbad on the
other side of the Nen Kalthar. To the east, you can barely make out the pool and
the dam beyond. You see nothing but miles of grassy, rolling plains south of
here. There is a small iron loop by the eastern edge of the battlements.
name: On the Castle Battlements

desc:
You enter an old watching tower. Parts of the walls are missing and a lot of
fissures run about in the remaining parts. Stairs lead up to what was the first
floor but look decidedly dangerous. Should you really venture on up?
name: The Old Tower

desc:
The city walls are quite old here and are promptly falling to pieces. You notice
some clefts in it which some birds have made their homes. The street continues
west and south from here and to the east an old watching tower rises up.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
You are standing south of the northern gate of the city. The main street is
south of here. To the east and to the west, a small alley is going next to the
city walls.
name: Before the North Gate of Tharbad

desc:
The alley runs west and east past huge city walls. Some bricks at the top of the
wall crumble, but overall it is in good condition. Rubbish is littering the
ground and a faint smell of putrification lies in the air. Not far to the east
an old tower connects to the city walls and reaches far into the sky, while
westwards an intersection of streets is visible.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
The street hugs the city walls here. The north gate is just west of here. To the
south you see a strange small house with dark and gloomy windows. They seem to
look back at you. The street leads east and west.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
You are just inside the northern gate of the city. Overhead a crudely shaped but
sturdy-looking ledge joins the two sections of the city wall on your east and
west. A guard post has been set up here to screen all who enter the city.
name: Inside the North Gate of Tharbad

desc:
You are walking next to the northern city wall. A disgusting smell is coming
from the west where the street continues further. To the east you see the north
gate of the city.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
The smell is really awful here. Big piles of rubbish have been thrown against
the wall to the north. To the south the houses are looking so old and abandoned
that you are afraid to see them falling on you when you step along them. The
street is going on further to the west and to the east.
name: The Old Tower Street

desc:
You are standing on a small street following the wall surrounding Tharbad. South
of here is West Alley and to the east the street is leading to the north gate of
the city.
name: Northwest Corner of the Wall

desc:
A heavy stench lies here, originating from animal droppings all over the floor.
Filthy twisting cracks and natural passages lead in most directions from this
cave. Only one leads further than a few yards before it becomes impassable
though, heading north. The walls are of brittle rock, and a large mound of it is
up against the southern end. The roof seems to have collapsed there, leaving a
huge dark hole.
name: Cramped Cave Passage

desc:
Pillars and columns suggest this cave was once above water level but has been
drowned since. The floor is covered with mud and slime, as it is too dark for
any real vegetation to grow here. A slow current plays with you and the debris
floating around. Sometimes it is pushing you further into the cave, sometimes
dragging you out.
name: Deep in an Underwater Cave

desc:
This is a rather large underwater cave in the north bank of the Greyflood. A few
small fish have found respite here, hiding in the cracks and among the seaweed
that grows near the entrance. The water is black and stagnant, full of specks
and lumps of dirt and debris that float slowly about, obscuring your vision.
name: A Dark Underwater Cave

desc:
This is a rather deep section of the river. The water is dirty and blocks most
of the light from the surface off. The seaweed covered bottom slopes downward to
the south. The north bank rises abruptly in a rotten bluff of crumbling
limestone.
name: The River Bottom

desc:
This short, dark green grass gives stark contrast to the packed dirt road that
runs west and south of here. The vegetation feels soft and pleasant compared to
the hard road, and the ambient temperature here is a few degrees cooler, too.
Eastward, you begin to see some bushes, and a tiny hill rises up to the north.
name: Short Grass on the Side of the Road

desc:
The occasional thorny bushes that dot the land are surrounded by a thin carpet
of dark green grass. The grass finally begins to yield to the bushes where they
grow thicker east and north of here, but to the west and south not a single
shrub is to be seen.
name: Scattered Bushes amid the Grass

desc:
This wide earthy trench does not seem to be a naturally formed gully, for
neither the road to the west, nor the tiny hill east of here seem likely to
produce sufficient runoff to create such a channel. More likely, but less
savoury, is the possibility that some of the less honourable among the denizens
of the city of Tharbad have created it for their own nefarious purposes.
name: In a Shallow Ditch beside the Road

desc:
Though this tiny mound of grass covered dirt hardly qualifies as a hill, it does
give a view of the road a few miles to the west and south across a ditch and a
field of grass, respectively. The light brush rising up north of here does not
look so pleasant, but the rough bushes to the east look particularly uninviting.
name: On Top of a Small Hill

desc:
Between a hill and a thicket, you find yourself wandering in the rough. Dense
bushes stand so close together you hardly notice the long, thin grass growing at
your feet. While the bushes thin toward the south, north doesn't seem any more
inviting than where you stand.
name: In the Rough

desc:
Dense thickets of thorny bushes dominate the landscape with short grass being
the only other dominant vegetation. This brush seems to stretch on forever in
all directions. The bushes make it difficult to move with their pointed thorns
and sandpaper-like leaves tearing at your clothes.
name: Dense Brush

desc:
The land is mainly flat with bushes and short grass as the only vegetation. The
bushes are mostly white thorn and thorny bushes. A small hill rises up to the
south while the brush goes on in all other directions. The area seems quite
desolate but the road to Tharbad probably lies somewhere to the southwest.
name: Rough Bushes

desc:
Short stubby grass grows everywhere with only an occasional bush to break the
harmony. Looking towards the east the bushes suddenly grow bigger. The Road to
Tharbad runs close by to the west and can be accessed from here without any
problems. A shallow ditch starts here and runs towards the south.
name: Scattered Bushes

desc:
A few bushes are scattered all over the area but besides those the only plants
that grow here are various short grasses. Looking around the horizon the
landscape looks like this in every direction except to the east where the bushes
are larger and grow closer together. A trail can be seen to the west.
name: Sparse Vegetation

desc:
The trail bends in a south-west arch connecting the stables to the west with the
big roadway running to the south. The stables are housed in a tall wooden
building in two storeys. Grassy fields offering grazing to the mounts of the
people visiting flank the trail. Very little other vegetation can be seen.
name: Trail by the Stables

desc:
Tall bushes and shrubbery grow close together and make movement very difficult.
Razor-sharp leaves and thorns rip through clothes and leather and bruise the
skin. The brush seems to go on in all directions except to the west where the
bushes grow shorter and farther apart.
name: Bushes

desc:
Short green grass grows in between the dreary-looking thorny bushes. The bushes
have sharp leaves and needle like thorns making you want to avoid getting too
close to them. The bushes grow in every direction as far as the eye can see. The
area seems quite desolate with no signs of civilisation.
name: Thin Brush

desc:
A small trail leading north-south passes by Jasper's tavern to the west. The
trail is flanked by plains on the east side with short green grass making a
dense green carpet. A few bushes grow in spots, but little other vegetation
thrives here. The tavern is built with sturdy stonewalls and a wooden roof.
name: Behind Jasper's Tavern

desc:
A large wooden sign painted with red letters clearly informs you that the stone
building to the east is known as Jasper's Tavern. A wide, pebbled path comes in
from the south, meandering towards the door of the tavern then continues north,
where it presumably goes to the back entrance. The short grass here is trampled
and patchy, with numerous footpaths leading east towards the tavern.
name: Outside a Noisy Tavern

desc:
Short green grass is the dominant plant life that abundantly covers the ground.
Little other vegetation is present, but small, dry bushes grow all around here
as well. The grass becomes taller to the north, and the bushes grow more dense
to the east. Due west, smoke rises from the chimney of a small stone building.
name: Plains

desc:
Small dry bushes and short green grass cover the ground in all directions. The
grass gets longer yet drier to the north, and the bushes become more scarce to
the west. The notable lack of trees makes the horizon seem empty. The dry land
seems quiet and rustic, as if it has not ever been touched by civilised hands.
name: Bushes

desc:
Dry coarse bushes grow in patches in the grassy fields. They have stiff, sharp
leaves covered with needle-like thorns giving them a natural defence against
plant eaters. This pattern goes on in all directions save north where the grass
seems to be getting taller and the ground drier.
name: Bushes

desc:
Though the water appears to be quite deep here, a number of large, fairly flat
boulders make it possible to walk around without wading or swimming. As the
river flows onward, the water laps and splashes against the rocks, making them
quite slippery, and a thin layer of moss can be seen growing on a few of the
tallest stones. Notably, the western part of this crossing has been washed out
by flood, and the tree above is too high to reach. However, you notice that the
the eastern bank looks easy enough to climb, as this was originally part of that
path.
name: Washed-Out River Crossing

desc:
The small hut looks cozy and comfortable. A small bed, table and some chairs are
all that furnishes the room, and a small window faces north. On one wall you can
see some of the ranger's small trophies, hung to either side of the plain,
wooden door, which is the only exit from this place.
name: Inside the Shed

desc:
The pool is wide and deep, and its silty bottom still lies some way below you.
The waters swirl lazily around, kicking up only a little of the sticky sediment,
and so they remain quite clear. Thick weeds grow throughout the pond, in places
so tall as to obstruct your view even this far from the bottom, but to the east,
at the wall of the dam, the area is surprisingly free from the tangle of
vegetation.
name: Deep in the Murky Waters

desc:
Although this chamber is deep within the dam, over which the waters of the Nen
Kalthar flow constantly, it is quite dry, and the air is fresh and sweet. In one
corner a small nest has been built, mainly from soft brown fur.
name: Inside the Dam

desc:
Covered with thick green grass, these plains continue far to the east of here.
To the south a wide road traverses the countryside, taking advantage of the
easily travelled terrain in this region. Further in the distance, in the west, a
sparkling river chooses its own meandering course through these comparatively
flat lands.
name: Grassy Plains

desc:
This must be the uppermost level of the castle. You can clearly see that the
many years of abandonment have taken their toll on this place. Three large,
round windows, facing north, south and west, are now empty of their glazing, and
a cold wind blows through the chamber. But they do afford a spectacular view of
the forest, which surrounds the castle.
name: Observatory

desc:
Nestling in the lee of a small hillock to the east, this quiet hollow is quite
sheltered, and fed by the waters of the nearby river its fertile soil is
carpeted by thick grass and wildflowers. A faint trail can barely be discerned
among the lush vegetation, but it seems that someone often travels north and
south through this area.
name: Quiet Hollow

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, but it seems that to the
north, the woodland thins out somewhat.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, and amid the dense, knotted
tangle of underbrush, any pathway is rapidly swallowed up, making it a difficult
task indeed to find a way through the woods.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, and amid the dense, knotted
tangle of underbrush, any pathway is rapidly swallowed up, making it a difficult
task indeed to find a way through the woods.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, but it seems that to the
south, the woodland thins out somewhat.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, and amid the dense, knotted
tangle of underbrush, any pathway is rapidly swallowed up, making it a difficult
task indeed to find a way through the woods.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
This forest must be one of the last remnants of a far greater woodland, which
once covered all Minhiriath, before the devastating deforestations by the men of
Numenor, who sought wood for their shipwrights, and to build their haven at Lond
Daer, far away on the coast. Tall and ancient trees completely surround you,
their long branches forming a leafy canopy overhead, and amid the dense, knotted
tangle of underbrush, any pathway is rapidly swallowed up, making it a difficult
task indeed to find a way through the woods.
name: Deep in the Forest

desc:
A few scattered trees mark the border of a forest, which stretches north from
here, rapidly thickening. Though thinly spread, the trees here are old and
gnarled, yet still strong and healthy. Perhaps this is one of the few remnants
of the massive forests which once covered the lands, before they were laid bare
to provide wood for the mighty Numenorean fleets.
name: Forest Border

desc:
An old stone well stands at the centre of a small glade of trees. In contrast to
the ancient woodland north of here, these trees seem to have been planted and
cultivated on purpose, though they appear to have been left untended for many
years, judging by the thick weeds which have sprung up around them. The well
itself is surrounded by a broad pathway of heavy stone paving, but even this is
in a poor state of repair, with many of the blocks cracked and weeds poking up
between them.
name: At the Old Well

desc:
This small hill rises only a little above the height of the surrounding lands,
but it nonetheless offers a commanding view of the countryside, as the terrain
is low and flat in all directions. A small river flows by to the east of here,
its waters keeping the land lush and fertile, and further in the distance a wide
road can be seen.
name: At the Top of a Small Hillock

desc:
The river is surrounded by leafy bushes and weeds, which plunge some of their
stalks and branches low, creating small whirling eddies in the current. The
water here is deep, but very clear, so that it is still possible to see the
riverbed, but further to the east the river grows wider and shallower, with
large rocks and boulders breaking the surface of the water, creating waves and
small sections of whitewater.
name: Sparkling Waters of a Small River

desc:
A soft, dim light fills this cavern, casting dancing shadows on the dirt and
litter, which are spread all over the floor. In the centre of the room, the
ashes of a large fire remain, within a ragged circle of stones, and next to this
stands a crude but functional table, surrounded by eight wooden chairs. Along
the south and west walls, a few niches have been cut out of the hard stone, to
offer more secluded sleeping places for the occupants of this cave.
name: Smugglers' Hiding Place

desc:
The faint trail you have been following ends here in a small valley, and you can
only travel back to the east. At the end of the valley a small mountain spring
wells from between two rocks, and drips slowly into a pool of water, ice cold
and crystal clear. To the east of here a large hill rises above the rest, and
from there, you could probably get a good view of the surrounding country- side.
name: High Hills

desc:
A faint grassy trail leads onward through the upland, rising and falling with
the rolling hills. From here, little of the surrounding country can be seen, as
the trail has found its way into a small grassy hollow, which nestles deep
between the surrounding hilltops. However, at least this coomb offers some
shelter from the unrelenting winds.
name: High Hills

desc:
It is hard to imagine anyone living in these high hills, and yet just east of
here, a small, ramshackle, wooden building can be seen. A rough trail through
the grass leads to the doorway, suggesting that this area still sees regular
travel. Far to the north, the silver ribbon of the Nen Kalthar winds gently east
and south across the land.
name: High Hills

desc:
This is a small chamber, only a few paces across in either direction, but much
has been stored here. Some paintings hang upon the walls, and a fine statue
stands in the far corner. A massive piece, this statue dominates the room, with
its big sword and a shield with the head of a lion in the middle, it is indeed a
shame that this is all but part of a sculpture, and not an ornate set of armour,
which would lend style to any warrior.
name: Treasure Room

desc:
This enormous felled tree now spans the Nen Kalthar where the old rock crossing
that linked the path from the forest to the Road to Tharbad used to be. Since a
flood washed out the western half of the crossing, this slippery tree serves as
the only bridge across the river for miles. Below, you can hear the waters
gurgling past the rocks of the old crossing.
name: On the Mighty Tree Bridge

desc:
The stone floor of this tower is very old and in poor repair, and provides
unstable footing. Plains are visible to the north, bisected by a well-travelled
road. The city lies to the south-west with all its small and dark houses, split
into two parts by the Greyflood.
name: Up in the Tower

desc:
It is hard to breathe in here, as the air is moist and holds a stench of animal
droppings. Winding twisting passages leave this cave in all directions, as if
the air itself was trying to escape. The only way that is not a dead end is
south. In the centre of the room is a wide pool of clear dark water. The surface
is pulsating slowly, as if something was moving down there - rising and sinking.
name: A Damp Cave

desc:
Flowing from north to south here, the river continues its journey through these
almost flat lands, its course a leisurely and meandering one. The low banks to
both east and west appear easily scaled should one wish to leave the water, but
further downstream the land does rise just a little above the level of the
channel. To the east, the bank is topped by a massive, flat, moss-covered stone,
which lies at an angle, so that its edge barely clears the waterline.
name: On a Small, Meandering River

desc:
The road bends sharply to the south and west here, avoiding the wooden building
to the east that blocks movement in that direction. A trail winds its way north
alongside the building. Looking south into the distance, a gauntlet of trees
borders the roadside. To the west the road opens out more as the trees begin to
thin.
name: Road to Tharbad
